children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A group health plan and a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage may not impose any preexisting condition exclusion with respect to such plan or coverage.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “preexisting condition exclusion” means, with respect to coverage, a limitation or exclusion of benefits relating to a condition based on the fact that the condition was present before the date of enrollment for such coverage, whether or not any medical advice, diagnosis, care, or treatment was recommended or received before such date.

children.heading: Treatment of genetic information
children.label: B Treatment of genetic information
children.text: Genetic information shall not be treated as a condition described in subsection (a)(1) in the absence of a diagnosis of the condition related to such information.1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Enrollment date
children.label: 2 Enrollment date
children.text: The term “enrollment date” means, with respect to an individual covered under a group health plan or health insurance coverage, the date of enrollment of the individual in the plan or coverage or, if earlier, the first day of the waiting period for such enrollment.

children.label: A
children.text: the first period in which the individual is eligible to enroll under the plan, or

children.label: B
children.text: a special enrollment period under subsection (f).

children.heading: Waiting period
children.label: 4 Waiting period
children.text: The term “waiting period” means, with respect to a group health plan and an individual who is a potential participant or beneficiary in the plan, the period that must pass with respect to the individual before the individual is eligible to be covered for benefits under the terms of the plan.

children.heading: Rules relating to crediting previous coverage
children.label: c Rules relating to crediting previous coverage
A
children.text: A group health plan.

children.label: B
children.text: Health insurance coverage.

children.label: C
children.text: Part A or part B of title XVIII of the Social Security Act [ et seq., 1395j et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1395c

children.label: D
children.text: Title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], other than coverage consisting solely of benefits under section 1928 [].42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1396s

children.label: E
children.text: Chapter 55 of title 10.

children.label: F
children.text: A medical care program of the Indian Health Service or of a tribal organization.

children.label: G
children.text: A State health benefits risk pool.

children.label: H
children.text: A health plan offered under chapter 89 of title 5.

children.label: I
children.text: A public health plan (as defined in regulations).

children.label: J
children.text: A health benefit plan under .section 2504(e) of title 22

children.heading: Not counting periods before significant breaks in coverage
children.label: 2 Not counting periods before significant breaks in coverage

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: A period of creditable coverage shall not be counted, with respect to enrollment of an individual under a group or individual health plan, if, after such period and before the enrollment date, there was a 63-day period during all of which the individual was not covered under any creditable coverage.

children.heading: Waiting period not treated as a break in coverage
children.label: B Waiting period not treated as a break in coverage
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A) and subsection (d)(4), any period that an individual is in a waiting period for any coverage under a group or individual health plan (or for group health insurance coverage) or is in an affiliation period (as defined in subsection (g)(2)) shall not be taken into account in determining the continuous period under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: TAA pre-certification period rule
children.label: i TAA pre-certification period rule
children.text: In the case of a TAA-eligible individual, the period beginning on the date the individual has a TAA-related loss of coverage and ending on the date that is 7 days after the date of the issuance by the Secretary (or by any person or entity designated by the Secretary) of a qualified health insurance costs credit eligibility certificate for such individual for purposes of shall not be taken into account in determining the continuous period under subparagraph (A).section 7527 of title 26

children.heading: Definitions
children.label: ii Definitions
children.text: The terms “TAA-eligible individual” and “TAA-related loss of coverage” have the meanings given such terms in .section 300bb–5(b)(4) of this title

children.heading: Standard method
children.label: A Standard method
children.text: Except as otherwise provided under subparagraph (B), for purposes of applying subsection (a)(3), a group health plan, and a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage, shall count a period of creditable coverage without regard to the specific benefits covered during the period.1

children.heading: Election of alternative method
children.label: B Election of alternative method
children.text: A group health plan, or a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance, may elect to apply subsection (a)(3) based on coverage of benefits within each of several classes or categories of benefits specified in regulations rather than as provided under subparagraph (A). Such election shall be made on a uniform basis for all participants and beneficiaries. Under such election a group health plan or issuer shall count a period of creditable coverage with respect to any class or category of benefits if any level of benefits is covered within such class or category.1

children.label: i
children.text: prominently state in any disclosure statements concerning the plan, and state to each enrollee at the time of enrollment under the plan, that the plan has made such election, and

children.label: ii
children.text: include in such statements a description of the effect of this election.

children.label: i
children.text: shall prominently state in any disclosure statements concerning the coverage, and to each employer at the time of the offer or sale of the coverage, that the issuer has made such election, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall include in such statements a description of the effect of such election.

children.heading: Establishment of period
children.label: 4 Establishment of period
children.text: Periods of creditable coverage with respect to an individual shall be established through presentation of certifications described in subsection (e) or in such other manner as may be specified in regulations.

children.heading: Exclusion not applicable to certain newborns
children.label: 1 Exclusion not applicable to certain newborns
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), a group health plan, and a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage, may not impose any preexisting condition exclusion in the case of an individual who, as of the last day of the 30-day period beginning with the date of birth, is covered under creditable coverage.

children.heading: Exclusion not applicable to certain adopted children
children.label: 2 Exclusion not applicable to certain adopted children
children.text: Subject to paragraph (4), a group health plan, and a health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage, may not impose any preexisting condition exclusion in the case of a child who is adopted or placed for adoption before attaining 18 years of age and who, as of the last day of the 30-day period beginning on the date of the adoption or placement for adoption, is covered under creditable coverage. The previous sentence shall not apply to coverage before the date of such adoption or placement for adoption.

children.heading: Exclusion not applicable to pregnancy
children.label: 3 Exclusion not applicable to pregnancy
children.text: A group health plan, and health insurance issuer offering group or individual health insurance coverage, may not impose any preexisting condition exclusion relating to pregnancy as a preexisting condition.

children.heading: Loss if break in coverage
children.label: 4 Loss if break in coverage
children.text: Paragraphs (1) and (2) shall no longer apply to an individual after the end of the first 63-day period during all of which the individual was not covered under any creditable coverage.

children.heading: Certifications and disclosure of coverage
children.label: e Certifications and disclosure of coverage

children.heading: Requirement for certification of period of creditable coverage
children.label: 1 Requirement for certification of period of creditable coverage
i
children.text: at the time an individual ceases to be covered under the plan or otherwise becomes covered under a COBRA continuation provision,

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of an individual becoming covered under such a provision, at the time the individual ceases to be covered under such provision, and

children.label: iii
children.text: on the request on behalf of an individual made not later than 24 months after the date of cessation of the coverage described in clause (i) or (ii), whichever is later.

children.label: i
children.text: the period of creditable coverage of the individual under such plan and the coverage (if any) under such COBRA continuation provision, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the waiting period (if any) (and affiliation period, if applicable) imposed with respect to the individual for any coverage under such plan.

children.heading: Issuer compliance
children.label: C Issuer compliance
children.text: To the extent that medical care under a group health plan consists of group health insurance coverage, the plan is deemed to have satisfied the certification requirement under this paragraph if the health insurance issuer offering the coverage provides for such certification in accordance with this paragraph.

children.heading: Disclosure of information on previous benefits
children.label: 2 Disclosure of information on previous benefits
A
children.text: upon request of such plan or issuer, the entity which issued the certification provided by the individual shall promptly disclose to such requesting plan or issuer information on coverage of classes and categories of health benefits available under such entity’s plan or coverage, and

children.label: B
children.text: such entity may charge the requesting plan or issuer for the reasonable cost of disclosing such information.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: 3 Regulations
children.text: The Secretary shall establish rules to prevent an entity’s failure to provide information under paragraph (1) or (2) with respect to previous coverage of an individual from adversely affecting any subsequent coverage of the individual under another group health plan or health insurance coverage.

children.label: A
children.text: The employee or dependent was covered under a group health plan or had health insurance coverage at the time coverage was previously offered to the employee or dependent.

children.label: B
children.text: The employee stated in writing at such time that coverage under a group health plan or health insurance coverage was the reason for declining enrollment, but only if the plan sponsor or issuer (if applicable) required such a statement at such time and provided the employee with notice of such requirement (and the consequences of such requirement) at such time.

children.label: i
children.text: was under a COBRA continuation provision and the coverage under such provision was exhausted; or

children.label: ii
children.text: was not under such a provision and either the coverage was terminated as a result of loss of eligibility for the coverage (including as a result of legal separation, divorce, death, termination of employment, or reduction in the number of hours of employment) or employer contributions toward such coverage were terminated.

children.label: D
children.text: Under the terms of the plan, the employee requests such enrollment not later than 30 days after the date of exhaustion of coverage described in subparagraph (C)(i) or termination of coverage or employer contribution described in subparagraph (C)(ii).

children.label: i
children.text: a group health plan makes coverage available with respect to a dependent of an individual,

children.label: ii
children.text: the individual is a participant under the plan (or has met any waiting period applicable to becoming a participant under the plan and is eligible to be enrolled under the plan but for a failure to enroll during a previous enrollment period), and

children.label: iii
children.text: a person becomes such a dependent of the individual through marriage, birth, or adoption or placement for adoption,

children.label: i
children.text: the date dependent coverage is made available, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the date of the marriage, birth, or adoption or placement for adoption (as the case may be) described in subparagraph (A)(iii).

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of marriage, not later than the first day of the first month beginning after the date the completed request for enrollment is received;

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a dependent’s birth, as of the date of such birth; or

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a dependent’s adoption or placement for adoption, the date of such adoption or placement for adoption.

children.heading: Special rules for application in case of Medicaid and CHIP
children.label: 3 Special rules for application in case of Medicaid and CHIP

children.heading: Termination of Medicaid or CHIP coverage
children.label: i Termination of Medicaid or CHIP coverage
children.text: The employee or dependent is covered under a Medicaid plan under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] or under a State child health plan under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.] and coverage of the employee or dependent under such a plan is terminated as a result of loss of eligibility for such coverage and the employee requests coverage under the group health plan (or health insurance coverage) not later than 60 days after the date of termination of such coverage.42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa

children.heading: Eligibility for employment assistance under Medicaid or CHIP
children.label: ii Eligibility for employment assistance under Medicaid or CHIP
children.text: The employee or dependent becomes eligible for assistance, with respect to coverage under the group health plan or health insurance coverage, under such Medicaid plan or State child health plan (including under any waiver or demonstration project conducted under or in relation to such a plan), if the employee requests coverage under the group health plan or health insurance coverage not later than 60 days after the date the employee or dependent is determined to be eligible for such assistance.

children.heading: Coordination with Medicaid and CHIP
children.label: B Coordination with Medicaid and CHIP

children.heading: Outreach to employees regarding availability of Medicaid and CHIP coverage
children.label: i Outreach to employees regarding availability of Medicaid and CHIP coverage

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Each employer that maintains a group health plan in a State that provides medical assistance under a State Medicaid plan under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], or child health assistance under a State child health plan under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.], in the form of premium assistance for the purchase of coverage under a group health plan, shall provide to each employee a written notice informing the employee of potential opportunities then currently available in the State in which the employee resides for premium assistance under such plans for health coverage of the employee or the employee’s dependents. For purposes of compliance with this subclause, the employer may use any State-specific model notice developed in accordance with .42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aasection 1181(f)(3)(B)(i)(II) of title 29

children.heading: Option to provide concurrent with provision of plan materials to employee
children.label: II Option to provide concurrent with provision of plan materials to employee
children.text: An employer may provide the model notice applicable to the State in which an employee resides concurrent with the furnishing of materials notifying the employee of health plan eligibility, concurrent with materials provided to the employee in connection with an open season or election process conducted under the plan, or concurrent with the furnishing of the summary plan description as provided in .section 1024(b) of title 29

children.heading: Disclosure about group health plan benefits to States for Medicaid and CHIP eligible individuals
children.label: ii Disclosure about group health plan benefits to States for Medicaid and CHIP eligible individuals
children.text: In the case of an enrollee in a group health plan who is covered under a Medicaid plan of a State under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] or under a State child health plan under title XXI of such Act [ et seq.], the plan administrator of the group health plan shall disclose to the State, upon request, information about the benefits available under the group health plan in sufficient specificity, as determined under regulations of the Secretary of Health and Human Services in consultation with the Secretary that require use of the model coverage coordination disclosure form developed under section 311(b)(1)(C) of the Children’s Health Insurance Reauthorization Act of 2009, so as to permit the State to make a determination (under paragraph (2)(B), (3), or (10) of section 2105(c) of the Social Security Act [, (3), (10)] or otherwise) concerning the cost-effectiveness of the State providing medical or child health assistance through premium assistance for the purchase of coverage under such group health plan and in order for the State to provide supplemental benefits required under paragraph (10)(E) of such section or other authority.42 U.S.C. 139642 U.S.C. 1397aa342 U.S.C. 1397ee(c)(2)(B)So in original. Probably should be followed by the word “Program”.3

children.heading: Use of affiliation period by HMOs as alternative to preexisting condition exclusion
children.label: g Use of affiliation period by HMOs as alternative to preexisting condition exclusion
A
children.text: such period is applied uniformly without regard to any health status-related factors; and

children.label: B
children.text: such period does not exceed 2 months (or 3 months in the case of a late enrollee).

children.heading: “Affiliation period” defined
children.label: A “Affiliation period” defined
children.text: For purposes of this subchapter, the term “affiliation period” means a period which, under the terms of the health insurance coverage offered by the health maintenance organization, must expire before the health insurance coverage becomes effective. The organization is not required to provide health care services or benefits during such period and no premium shall be charged to the participant or beneficiary for any coverage during the period.

children.heading: Beginning
children.label: B Beginning
children.text: Such period shall begin on the enrollment date.

children.heading: Runs concurrently with waiting periods
children.label: C Runs concurrently with waiting periods
children.text: An affiliation period under a plan shall run concurrently with any waiting period under the plan.

children.heading: Alternative methods
children.label: 3 Alternative methods
children.text: A health maintenance organization described in paragraph (1) may use alternative methods, from those described in such paragraph, to address adverse selection as approved by the State insurance commissioner or official or officials designated by the State to enforce the requirements of this part for the State involved with respect to such issuer.
